verdict.answer_first: B states the actionable answer more completely in its first section by including the crucial `sudo` hint immediately: "then re-run with sudo if it prints nothing"
verdict.decisiveness: B is more decisive, framing the solution as "The single command that settles it" and providing a direct one-liner kill command: `kill $(lsof -ti:<port>)`
verdict.fabrication: A invents a specific detail about my personal machine setup: "*this is very likely on your machine specifically*, since your Mac runs a PM2/launchd bridge"
verdict.actionability: B is more actionable because it anticipates the common failure of `lsof` needing `sudo` and provides a copy-paste command `kill $(lsof -ti:<port>)` to immediately solve the most likely problem.
